sanskrit:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम्।
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः॥
transliteration:
agnir jyotir ahaḥ śuklaḥ ṣaṇ-māsā uttarāyaṇam
 tatra prayātā gacchanti brahma brahma-vido janāḥ
literal_meaning: Fire, light, day, the bright [fortnight], the six months of the northern path—those who depart then, knowers of Brahman, go to Brahman.
contextual_meaning: The psyche experiences a symbolic ‘north-bound’ window when cognitive filters thin and the Self can dis-identify from personal history; consciously choosing this ‘exit’ moment (death of an old identity) allows integration with the trans-personal ground of awareness rather than recycling the same egoic patterns.
psychological_state: A heightened sense of agency in the face of major life transition; the belief that if one ‘dies’ (to a role, relationship, or story) at the right symbolic moment, the passage can be evolutionary rather than repetitive.
trigger_scenario: Standing at the crossroads of a career change or divorce, sensing that timing and mindset will decide whether you repeat the same dynamics or step into an expanded identity.
anchor_text: Leave the old self in clarity, not confusion, and merge with the light beyond form.
